claim: The post-9/11 United States sacred political order responded to the attacks through sacred-enemy escalation: the AUMF granted the executive unlimited force authorization against a broadly defined enemy, while Bush's congressional address constructed a globally totalized binary — 'with us or with the terrorists' — that sacralized the US sacred political order through opposition to an absolute external threat, foreclosing the constitutional-containment alternative.

confidence.label: moderate-high
confidence.rationale: Both passages are public-law and public-address texts verified against official sources. The AUMF's open-ended authorization and Bush's binary framing are the canonical founding documents of the post-9/11 order. Confidence capped pending chain expansion including Patriot Act, drone campaign, and torture program documentation.
